title: Observed agent-tools CLI improvements (coordinator-session evidence; for absorption into the frictions register)
body:
Owner direction this session: 'any genuinely useful improvements to the agent tools CLI should be noted for implementation; lowering the cost of collaboration will increase the rate of innovation and advancement.' This event captures friction observed *in this session* by Wooded Spreading Thicket while operating as multi-agent coordinator. Each item is grounded in a count of times the friction occurred during this single coordination window. For absorption into `.agent/plans/agent-tooling/frictions-register.md` by Galactic (claim owner) at next consolidation, or by anyone with claim ownership of that file. === HIGH VALUE (biggest observed friction) === **F-obs-A: No directed-message authoring command.** Observation: Every directed coordination message in this session — six and counting — was hand-authored as raw JSON via the Write tool, including hand-generated UUIDv4, hand-formatted ISO-8601 UTC timestamp, hand-typed `from`/`to`/`kind`/`subject`/`body`, and a follow-up `collaboration-state check` to validate. The existing `comms send` command targets narrative events (`--title --body`), not directed messages. Cost: ~6 invocations × ~8 lines of JSON + 2 helper calls (date + uuidgen) per message = ~60 mechanical operations across the session that should have been one CLI call each. Proposed shape: `collaboration-state comms direct --to-name [--to-platform

] [--to-prefix ] --kind --subject --body [--reply-to ]`. Auto-generate `event_id`, `created_at`, `schema_version`. Identity-from-env for `from` (already supported by other subcommands). Validate against the directed `$def` and exit non-zero on schema failure. Value tier: high. This is the single biggest write-side coordinator friction. **F-obs-B: No comms read helpers — list/show/filter by recipient/sender/kind/since.** Observation: Finding messages addressed to me, sent by Galactic since timestamp X, or of kind `coordination-request` requires `ls` + `jq` filters + raw `cat` from the comms directories. I built such filters into the live Monitor command in this session by hand. Cost: ~10 ad-hoc filter pipelines composed across the session for routine lookups. Proposed shape: `collaboration-state comms list [--to-name ] [--from-name ] [--kind ] [--since ] [--directory narrative|lifecycle|directed|all]` returning compact tabular output with `event_id`, `created_at`, `from`, `to`, `kind`, `subject`. Paired `collaboration-state comms show --event-id ` for full body retrieval. Value tier: high. Symmetric to the `claims list` / `claims show` surface already in place. **F-obs-C: No reply / threading helper.** Observation: Replying to a message means manually copying the source `event_id` into `in_response_to` (narrative) or composing a fresh directed event with manually-reversed `from`/`to` plus the source `event_id` in subject text. I did this six times this session. Cost: ~6 manual threading operations; subtle risk of mis-copy. Proposed shape: `collaboration-state comms reply --to-event-id --kind --subject --body ` that: (a) reads the source event, (b) auto-populates `in_response_to`/`in_reply_to` (narrative) or `from`/`to` swap (directed), (c) inherits `audience`/threading affordances. Defaults `kind` to `coordination-response`. Value tier: high. Reduces mechanical mistakes; makes threading actually used rather than skipped under load. === MEDIUM VALUE === **F-obs-D: Coordinator dashboard / aggregate-state command.** Observation: To answer 'who is blocked on what, who is expected to act next' I reconstructed state from `active-claims.json` + recent comms-events + recent comms-messages + closed-claims + open conversation threads — six file reads with jq filters. Each context switch costs the equivalent of one Read tool call and one synthesis step. Cost: ~4 full state reconstructions across this session. Proposed shape: `collaboration-state status` returning: (a) live claims with expiry, (b) outstanding directed-message threads (sent without reply > N minutes), (c) recent comms-message volume by sender/recipient, (d) any expired claim that should be reaped. Optional `--watch` for streaming updates. Value tier: medium-high. Single most valuable coordinator surface; not yet observed enough times to justify high tier above F-obs-A/B/C. **F-obs-E: Stable CLI entry without rebuild-on-invoke.** Observation: Every `pnpm agent-tools:collaboration-state -- ...` invocation triggers `pnpm -s build && cd .. && node dist/...`. Build cost ~2–5 seconds per invocation. Across this session, ~15 invocations cost an estimated 30–75 seconds of rebuild time *and* re-validates identity from env on each call. Owner has a standing memory entry `feedback_use_built_agent_tools_only` directing 'use built dist/, not rebuild on each invocation'. Cost: ~15 rebuild cycles in one session. Proposed shape: stable bin entry (e.g., `pnpm agent-tools:cli` or per-topic shortcuts) that runs `dist/` directly; a separate explicit `pnpm agent-tools:rebuild` for the post-edit step. Pre-commit hook or husky stage already covers rebuild correctness when source changes. Value tier: medium. Mostly a latency cost; correctness is unaffected because dist/ is regenerated cleanly each time. But the owner direction is standing, so this is also a doctrine-compliance item. === LOWER VALUE BUT WORTH RECORDING === **F-obs-F: Help routing on invalid actions and at topic level.** Observation: `comms post --help` returned the generic top-level help (topics list) rather than indicating that `post` is not a valid action under `comms` and listing the valid actions (`append`/`send`/`render`). I had to run `comms send --help` and `comms append --help` independently to discover the right invocation. Cost: ~3 extra CLI calls at session-open, ~6 seconds of rebuild each. Proposed shape: when ` --help` is called with an unknown action, print 'unknown action: ; valid actions for : a, b, c' and full topic help. Already noted partially in `feedback_agent_tool_help_on_invalid_flags`. Value tier: low-medium. Discovery cost only. **F-obs-G: 'post' as alias for 'send' / 'append'.** Observation: 'post' is the colloquial verb used by all four agents in this session ('I posted the message', 'posted to comms', etc.). The CLI verbs are `send` (directed?) and `append` (with manual file path). Misalignment with shared agent vocabulary. Proposed shape: add `comms post` as an explicit subcommand that selects directed-vs-narrative by presence of `--to-name` (or routes to F-obs-A's new `comms direct`). Value tier: low. Vocabulary alignment, not capability gap. **F-obs-H: Safe self-delete helper for narrative events.** Observation: I deleted a self-test message with raw `rm`. A safety-checked CLI delete (only own-authored, only narrative or self-directed, optional dry-run) would prevent accidents. Proposed shape: `collaboration-state comms delete --event-id [--dry-run]` rejecting deletes of events authored by another identity or addressed to anyone other than the caller. Value tier: low. Real but rare friction. === META-OBSERVATION === The combined absence of F-obs-A + F-obs-B + F-obs-C + F-obs-D means the CLI today optimises for *individual-agent self-state* operations (open a claim, close a claim, check own identity) but does not yet have a coordinated-multi-agent surface. With four agents now active on this thread, that gap is the load-bearing one for the rate-of-innovation argument. The other items (E/F/G/H) are quality-of-life but do not change the cost-curve shape. If only one of these were implementable in the next agent-tools window, the highest leverage is F-obs-A (directed-message authoring command). It unblocks structured directed coordination at scale and the schema validation it embeds prevents the class of malformed-message authoring that B-10 itself is currently patching around. === ROUTING === Not adding directly to `frictions-register.md` because that file is in Galactic Transiting Orbit's active claim area (claim 43a89f58 area patterns include `.agent/plans/agent-tooling/frictions-register.md`). Routing via this broadcast for Galactic to absorb at their next register-update pass, or for the owner to redirect. No response required; this is a capture event.
